problem_statement: After maintenance on a shore gateway, command messages to a subset of marine research buoys time out during connection setup while telemetry from those buoys continues to arrive. The maintenance changed more than one delivery input and did not retain a comparable effective pre-change snapshot. Determine the defensible diagnostic posture and a read-only way to isolate the failing edge.

observations.statement: Failures are confined to command downlinks routed through gateway west and listener relay-v2; telemetry uplinks and command downlinks through gateway east remain healthy.
observations.provenance: message route ledger and gateway counters

observations.statement: A retained passive trace shows the affected connection sending a server-name value for relay-west.internal and receiving an unrecognized-name alert before any command bytes are transmitted.
observations.provenance: redacted packet capture from the incident window

observations.statement: The current desired template names commands.ocean.example, and the presented certificate covers that name but not relay-west.internal.
observations.provenance: configuration repository and read-only certificate inspection

observations.statement: The gateway renders its listener configuration from the template plus a region-alias environment value, but maintenance records preserve neither the old value nor the rendered pre-change output.
observations.provenance: deployment template and maintenance evidence inventory

observations.statement: The maintenance window also updated the connection library used by the command sidecar; its archived notes describe a fallback to the dial target when no explicit server name is supplied.
observations.provenance: signed software inventory and vendor notes archive

observations.statement: Only redacted checksums of effective gateway output were retained, and serializer changes make the old and current checksums incomparable even when semantically equivalent inputs are reconstructed.
observations.provenance: configuration attestation records and serializer migration note

observations.statement: Two isolated reconstructions from archived material both fit the observed trace: an omitted rendered server-name field with client fallback, and an explicit field rendered from the unavailable alias value.
observations.provenance: offline gateway and sidecar reconstruction report

observations.statement: DNS answers, certificate validity, satellite-link quality, queue age, and buoy firmware are normal; none can explain why failure precedes application data only on one listener.
observations.provenance: read-only infrastructure and device health bundle

observations.statement: The gateway can expose a redacted effective-listener dump, and the sidecar can log whether server name was explicit or derived, both correlated to a passive handshake identifier without sending a buoy command.
observations.provenance: administrative endpoint schema and sidecar trace capability review
